ad.title: Senior Product Designer
ad.description:
Role Summary
​We need a strong product designer to drive end-to-end UX/UI for the ELSA mobile app, working tightly with a Product team in India and Design leadership in New York. The job is to ship high-quality, measurable improvements fast.
​Key Responsibilities
​Own design for key product areas: (typical: onboarding, home, lesson flows, AI coaching surfaces, paywall/subscription, retention loops).
​Turn fuzzy problems into shippable solutions: user journeys, wireframes, prototypes, final UI, specs.
​Partner daily with PM + Engineering (India-based): define scope, constraints, and tradeoffs.
​Run lightweight discovery and validation: (Maze tests, usability tests, surveys, funnel review) and iterate based on evidence.
​Maintain and extend the design system: (components, patterns, accessibility, states).
​Ensure clean handoff: redlines/specs, edge cases, QA, post-launch check-ins.
​Communicate clearly in async: Looms, concise docs, decisions + rationale.
​What “good” looks like in 60–90 days
​You’ve shipped at least 1–2 meaningful improvements tied to a funnel metric (activation/retention/paid conversion).
​You run a tight design cycle with the India product team (weekly cadence, crisp handoffs, minimal churn).
​You proactively surface risks (scope creep, tech constraints, unclear success metrics) and propose options.
​What You Will Have
​3–7 years product design experience (consumer mobile strongly preferred).
​Strong interaction design + visual polish (you can design production-ready UI).
​Solid product thinking: metrics, tradeoffs, experimentation mindset.
​Comfortable with ambiguity and speed; you don’t need perfect inputs to start.
​Strong Figma skills (components, auto-layout, variants, prototyping).
​Excellent written communication and async collaboration habits.
​Experience working cross-timezone and with engineers.
​Nice to have
​Experience in edtech, language learning, subscriptions, or growth loops.
​Experience with AI-powered features (coaching, personalization, generation).
​Motion/micro-interactions, accessibility, and design systems depth.
​What to include in application
​Portfolio with 2–3 case studies showing: problem → exploration → shipped outcome (or strong validation).
​For at least one case study, show mobile UI detail and how you worked with Engineering.
​What We Offer
​Flexible work setup: Remote and hybrid work arrangements available.
​Comprehensive employee well-being benefits.
​Free ELSA Premium courses to polish your language skills.
​Collaborative, international team culture.
​Global Impact: Opportunity to contribute to a fast-growing, well-funded Silicon Valley startup.

ad.title: Senior Data Science Analyst
ad.description:
Role Summary
We are looking for a Senior Data Science Analyst to join our core Data team. This is a high-impact, generalist role designed for someone who thrives on ambiguity and can translate complex data into strategic decisions. You will initially be deployed to solve high-priority challenges in either B2C Marketing (optimizing acquisition and LTV) or B2B Customer Success (driving retention and product adoption). You won't just build dashboards; you will design the experimentation frameworks and predictive models that define our in-house data strategy.


Key Responsibilities:

  • Cross-Functional Analytics: Partner with B2C or B2B stakeholders to define KPIs, track performance, and identify growth levers.

  • Experimentation & Statsig Ownership: Design and analyze A/B tests to measure the impact of new features, pricing changes, or marketing campaigns.

  • Behavioral Modeling: Use Python/R to develop models for customer health, churn probability, or lead scoring to help the Success and Marketing teams prioritize their efforts.

  • Self-Serve Ecosystem: Build and maintain scalable data models in Snowflake and intuitive dashboards in Looker that empower non-technical teams to find their own answers.
    Strategic Storytelling: Synthesize raw data into clear narratives for leadership, providing actionable recommendations that go beyond "what happened" to "what we should do next."

What You Will Have:

  • Experience: 4–7+ years in Data Science, Product Analytics, or Growth Analytics.

  • Technical Stack: Expert SQL (Window functions, CTEs) and proficiency in Python or R for statistical modeling.

  • Statistical Rigor: Strong grasp of A/B testing, hypothesis testing, and causal inference.

  • Tooling: Experience with modern BI (Looker, Tableau) and Event Analytics (Amplitude, Mixpanel).

  • Communication: Proven ability to influence stakeholders (like Marketing or Success Leads) through data.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with Snowflake or similar cloud data warehouses.

  • Background in SaaS or Subscription business models.

  • Familiarity with Git-based workflows (dbt/GitHub).

ad.title: Machine Learning / Speech Engineer (Remote)
ad.description:
Role Summary
You will be joining the AI Research Team, a group of experts dedicated to advancing AI-driven language learning technologies.
We are seeking a skilled Applied Machine Learning Engineer to design, develop, and deploy the core machine learning systems that power ELSA's personalized language learning experience. This role focuses on:
* Enhancing our automated scoring systems for spoken English.
* Developing robust methods for tracking user proficiency across various skills.
* Contributing to the evolution of our adaptive learning algorithms.
The ideal candidate has a strong foundation in machine learning—ideally with experience in speech processing or NLP—and possesses excellent programming skills. You will work closely with researchers, data scientists, and engineers to produce scalable, production-ready ML systems that directly impact millions of learners.
Key Responsibilities
* Model Development: Develop and deploy machine learning models for ELSA's automated analysis of spoken proficiency.
* Proficiency Tracking: Build and maintain ML systems to accurately track and model user proficiency across various English language skills.
* Adaptive Learning: Contribute to the research and refinement of ELSA's adaptive learning system to create personalized learning paths.
* Evaluation Frameworks: Implement and manage robust frameworks to measure model performance, analyze user impact, and guide iterative improvements.
* Cross-functional Collaboration: Work with researchers, engineers, and product teams to integrate ML models seamlessly into the ELSA platform.
What You Will Have
Must-Haves
* ML Fundamentals: Solid understanding and practical experience with classification, regression, sequence modeling, and evaluation metrics.
* Domain Expertise: Strong background in ML, preferably with an academic or professional focus on speech processing or NLP.
* Technical Stack: Proficiency in Python and common libraries (scikit-learn, Pandas, NumPy, PyTorch/TensorFlow).
* Software Engineering: Strong skills including experience with APIs, databases, testing, and development best practices.
* Data Handling: Experience with preprocessing, feature engineering, and data analysis.
* Education: Master's or PhD in Computer Science, ML, Data Science, Electrical Engineering, or a related quantitative field (or equivalent practical experience).
Nice-to-Haves
* Speech Tech: Hands-on experience with ASR, TTS, or other speech processing techniques.
* NLP Applications: Experience with text classification, sequence tagging, or language modeling.
* LLMs: Experience using Large Language Models for classification or feature extraction.
* Cloud Infrastructure: Experience with cloud stacks (e.g., AWS—including DynamoDB and S3).
What We Offer
* Flexibility: Remote and hybrid work arrangements available.
* Well-being: Comprehensive employee well-being benefits.
* Growth: Free ELSA Premium courses to polish your own language skills.
* Culture: A collaborative, international team environment.
* Impact: The opportunity to contribute to a fast-growing, well-funded Silicon Valley startup with global impact.?

ad.title: AI Engineer (Remote)
ad.description:
Job overview:

Join the Gen AI & LLM Team to develop and deploy LLM-powered capabilities, enhancing ELSA’s conversational tutoring. This AI Engineer role bridges software engineering and machine learning, requiring a pragmatic, change-driving individual to accelerate AI development, evaluation, and deployment. You will collaborate with researchers and engineers to implement cutting-edge AI in scalable, real-world applications.

Key Responsibilities

Design, build, and deploy production-grade AI agents for conversational and task-oriented experiences.

Architect scalable agentic systems (memory, tool orchestration, multi-step workflows), ensuring reliability and impact.

Implement robust evaluation, observability, and feedback loops to drive continuous improvement in performance and cost efficiency.

Develop secure, interoperable tool integrations (APIs, external systems, structured retrieval) adhering to modern standards.

Collaborate with research, product, and engineering to translate AI capabilities into scalable, user-facing applications.

Integrate speech technologies (ASR/TTS) into conversational AI systems where applicable.

What You Will Have

Must-Haves:

Strong experience building and deploying AI systems powered by LLMs, with a focus on real-world reliability and user impact.

Solid understanding of agentic architectures, including tool orchestration, memory design, multi-step reasoning, and structured workflows.

Deep experience with multi-agentic memory systems, including episodic, semantic, and procedural memory design, shared memory across agents, memory consolidation strategies, and context window management in long-horizon tasks.

Experience integrating AI systems with external APIs, structured data sources, and retrieval systems in production environments.

Hands-on experience with MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration — including building, deploying, and managing MCP servers, tool/resource/prompt exposure patterns, and secure client-server communication in agentic pipelines.

Hands-on experience with evaluation, monitoring, and performance optimization of AI applications (latency, cost, robustness, safety).

Strong software engineering fundamentals, including distributed systems, APIs, containerization, and cloud-native deployment.

Practical knowledge of prompt design, model adaptation (fine-tuning or parameter-efficient approaches), and controlled generation techniques.

Understanding of security considerations in AI systems, including prompt injection risks, tool permission scoping, and data handling practices.

Experience designing and orchestrating multi-agent systems with role specialization, inter-agent communication protocols, and coordination patterns (supervisor, peer-to-peer, hierarchical).

Experience working cross-functionally with research, product, and engineering teams in fast-moving environments.

Nice-to-Have:

Experience with multi-agent systems, planner–executor architectures, or structured reasoning frameworks.

Familiarity with interoperability standards and tool communication protocols for agentic ecosystems.

Experience designing long-context or memory-augmented AI systems (episodic and semantic memory strategies).

Knowledge of advanced model optimization techniques, including parameter-efficient fine-tuning, distillation, or model compression.

Experience integrating AI systems with speech technologies (ASR and TTS) for real-time conversational applications.

Background in NLP, conversational AI, or education-focused AI products.

What We Offer

Flexible work setup: Remote and hybrid work arrangements available.

Comprehensive employee well-being benefits.

Free ELSA Premium courses to polish your language skills

Collaborative, international team culture.

Opportunity to contribute to a fast-growing, well-funded Silicon Valley startup with global impact.

ad.title: Mechanical Design Engineer
ad.description:
About the job


Job Description

  • Device solutions to mechanical problems at an assembly/ sub-assembly level.
  • Develop assembly and component drawings for parts to be manufactured.
  • Design wholistic test to inquire parameters in order to translate customer requirements into technical requirements.
  • Attach risk levels to solutions proposed in order to propose sound solutions.
  • Produce design documents, Test reports and other related documentation.
  • Support fabrication, welding, machining, and assembly processes
  • Structural analysis on component and sub-assembly level to ensure design specifications are met.
  • Select suitable materials, actuators, components, fasteners, etc to meet design requirement
  • Develop hydraulic circuits including pumps, valves, cylinders, hoses, and safety locks
  • Applies sound engineering principles, with innovation to meet set goals.
  • Build-up of prototypes/ POCs.


Requirement:

  • Bachelor's Degree in mechanical engineering or equivalent.
  • Minimum 2 years in mechanical engineering / mechanical component design.
  • Proficiency in CAD software (SolidWorks)
  • Design knowledge in sheet metal parts, weldments, truss structures, machine parts.
  • Design knowledge for ease of manufacture and assembly and ingress protection.
  • Design knowledge in motion control / Kinematic solutions.
  • FEA of quasistatic loading conditions.
  • Design knowledge in hydraulics will be of added advantages.
  • Experience in mechanical design of large structures, mobile platforms, containerized systems or vehicle-mounted equipment will be of added advantages

ad.title: Quality Assurance Engineer (Automation/Robotics)
ad.description:
Role:

A Quality Assurance Engineer at HOPE Technik is vital for maintaining the quality and reliability of products in robotics, automation, and special vehicle projects. This role involves hands-on work in testing, analyzing, and improving products to meet the required standards and specifications.


Key Responsibilities:


  1. Quality Testing and Validation:

    • Formulate a test plan and thoroughly test products and components to ensure they meet predefined quality standards.

    • Develop and implement testing protocols and procedures for various stages of product development.

    • Utilize both manual and automated testing techniques.


  1. Issue Identification and Resolution:

    • Identify any defects or inconsistencies in products or processes.

    • Collaborate with the product development team to understand product requirements and ensure quality considerations are integrated through out.

    • Provide actionable insights and recommendations for resolving quality issues.


  1. Continuous Improvement:

    • Participate in continuous improvement initiatives to enhance product quality and production efficiency.

    • Analyse data and feedback to identify patterns and areas for improvement.

    • Recommend modifications in products or processes based on findings and industry best practices.


  1. Documentation and Reporting:

    • Maintain accurate records of testing procedures, methodologies, and results.

    • Prepare detailed reports on quality assurance activities, findings, and project progress.

    • Ensure all documentation is compliant with industry standards and company policies.


  1. Collaboration and Communication:

    • Work closely with other engineering teams, including design, production, and software development, to integrate quality assurance into all phases of the product development lifecycle.

    • Communicate effectively with team members, management, and external stakeholders regarding quality metrics, objectives, and outcomes.

    • Participate in regular team meetings and provide updates on quality assurance activities.


Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Quality, or a related technical field.

  • Proven experience as a Quality Assurance Engineer or similar role, preferably in robotics, automation, or vehicle engineering.

  • Strong understanding of quality assurance methodologies, tools, and standards.

  • Experience with statistical analysis and quality testing tools.

  • Ability to troubleshoot and provide solutions to quality-related issues.

  • Excellent attention to detail, problem-solving skills, and organizational abilities.

  • Effective communication and teamwork skills.

  • Knowledge of industry-specific regulations and standards is a plus.

ad.title: Electrical & Electronics Engineer
ad.description:
Role:

The individual is part of the electrical team, excelling in electronics and electrical engineering. This role contributes to electrical control panel design, power electronics circuit design, and battery-powered applications. The specialization lies in electrical integration. This role typically collaborates on projects with teammates across multiple disciplines and is expected to maintain high levels of interaction as needed. This role is expected to deliver work conscientiously, meeting deadlines and staying within budget.


Responsibilities:

  • Conduct feasibility studies at the early stages of the project.

  • Derive electrical engineering specifications from customers' requirements

  • Selection and integration of IOT and power management components

  • Design electrical circuits, panels, harnesses and looms

  • Generate build/rework instructions for production team and resolve production issues

  • Create and execute tests for electrical systems

  • Perform troubleshooting for electrical systems

  • Create BOM for electrical designs


Requirements:

  • Degree in Electrical & Electronics Engineering

  • Minimum 2 years of relevant experience

  • Strong fundamentals in electrical and electronics engineering

  • Excellent knowledge in AC power electronics and its associated control circuitry

  • Ability to derive safety and functional requirements for electrical design

  • Familiar with industrial electrical standards

  • Able to read drawings, understand engineering documents, and work closely with members of the engineering, operations and purchasing team

  • Experience in computer-aided drafting and design with tools such as Altium and Eplan

  • Strong communication skills in a multi-disciplinary team

  • Good analytical and critical thinking skills to be able to quickly and correctly identify and understand problems to find suitable solutions

  • Proficient in using MS Office tools (Word, Excel, Powerpoint)

  • Ability to work on multiple projects simultaneously

ad.title: Assembly Operator (Contract)
ad.description:
Job Description:
• Assemble products according to specified procedures and quality standards.
• Operate and maintain assembly equipment and tools.
• Perform quality checks and report any defects or issues.
• Collaborate with team members to meet production targets and deadlines.


Job Requirements:
• Secondary education or equivalent; technical certification is an advantage.
• Previous experience in a manufacturing environment preferred.
• Hands-on experience in assembly line.
• Excellent teamwork skills and ability to work in a multicultural environment.

ad.title: Project Manager
ad.description:
About the job


The Project Manager will play a critical role in managing cross functional projects, coordinating with internal stakeholders and external vendors, and ensuring timely and high-quality delivery of key initiatives.

This individual will oversee portfolio timelines across multiple team functions to lead cross-subject project milestones with effective stakeholder management and communication on statues while taking ownership to drive efficient solutions to keep alignment with planned scope, deliverables, and expectations.

This role requires strong communication, stakeholder management, analytical thinking, and experience in highly regulated or technology driven environments—ideally within financial markets, exchanges, clearing or SaaS platforms.

The Project Manager plays a crucial role in overseeing and ensuring the successful delivery of projects within an organisation, focusing on both strategic and tactical aspects of project management.


Key Responsibilities

  • Develop, track, and manage detailed project plans, timelines, and milestones for multiple concurrent projects across streams.
  • Coordinate and streamline collaboration between internal departments (Technology, Product, Operations, Regulatory Compliance, Legal, etc.).
  • Ensure alignment between project requirements, resources, and deliverables.
  • Responsible for planning, executing and monitoring projects.
  • Serve as the primary liaison between Abaxx Exchange and external vendors, system integrators, and technology partners.
  • Manage vendor performance, deliverable quality, SLAs, and contractual obligations.
  • Lead discussions, negotiations, and escalation processes to resolve scope, timelines, technical challenges, or operational issues.
  • Develop UAT strategies, test plans, and acceptance criteria in collaboration with Product and Technology teams.
  • Identify project risks, constraints, and gaps early; propose mitigation strategies and solutions.
  • Facilitate issue resolution across internal teams and vendors.
  • Maintain accurate risk logs, issue trackers, and project documentation.
  • Prepare and deliver regular project status updates, dashboards, and executive briefings.
  • Ensure projects comply with governance processes, regulatory expectations, and internal audit requirements.
  • Maintain documentation such as BRDs, project plans, meeting minutes, and RAID logs.


Qualifications & Experience

  • Bachelor’s degree in business, Finance, Computer Science, Engineering, or related discipline.
  • 10-15 years of experience in project management, ideally within fintech, financial services, trading platforms, exchanges, clearing or technology implementation.
  • Proven experience managing vendors and cross-functional teams in complex, multi-stakeholder environments.
  • Strong knowledge of project management methodologies (Agile, Waterfall, Hybrid) with certification in relevant area.
  • Hands-on experience planning and executing UAT cycles.
  • Excellent communication, negotiation, and problem-solving skills.
  • High degree of ownership, follow-through, and operational discipline.
  • Possess excellent leadership skills to guide project teams and influence stakeholders effectively.

ad.title: Market Data Program Specialist
ad.description:
About the job


Market Data Program Specialist (Singapore / London)

We are seeking a commercially driven Market Data Program Specialist with strong technical expertise to lead the development, commercialization, and structural design of the exchange’s market data offering. This role sits at the intersection of product strategy, revenue growth, and market data technology, responsible for building scalable market data structures that support real-time and historical data distribution while maximizing commercial value.

The successful candidate will shape how market data is packaged, priced, delivered, and consumed by global clients, while working closely with technology teams to design and evolve the underlying market data architecture.

Key Responsibilities

Strategic development , Licensing Framework & Program Governance
  • Collaborate with management to shape the market data strategy in alignment with broader organisational objectives
  • Lead the development, refinement, and maintenance of a complex market data licensing program covering multiple license types, usage scenarios, and delivery formats.
  • Collaborate with Legal to formalise and update program terms & conditions, ensuring clarity, compliance, and enforceability.
  • Collaborate with Commercial to refine commercial structure of the program and development of the commercial business plan for the program
  • Assist in drafting and revising program agreements, data use policies, FAQs, and guidance documents for prospective and active licensees.
Licensee Management & Operational Support

  • Serve as the primary operational point of contact for licensees throughout the application, evaluation, and approval processes.
  • Manage and update required submission forms, including statements of intended data use, delivery method preferences, and compliance attestations.
  • Collaborate closely with Legal to negotiate terms, usage rights, data restrictions where applicable
  • Coordinate workflows across Legal, Technology and Product teams to ensure timely and accurate evaluation of licensing applications and delivery of connectivity

Onboarding & Data Delivery

  • Lead end-to-end onboarding for new licensees, including introduction to program requirements, delivery processes, and compliance expectations.
  • Partner with Technology (and Data Engineering) teams to facilitate secure and accurate delivery of licensed datasets and participate in planning for software/infrastructure development in relation to data delivery.
  • Develop onboarding checklists, communication templates, and documentation to improve operational consistency.

Process Improvement & Reporting

  • Identify gaps and inefficiencies in licensing workflows, proposing and implementing improvements to streamline processing time and accuracy.
  • Maintain internal documentation on licensing rules, data products, delivery pathways, and escalation procedures.
  • Produce regular reports on licensing volume, operational metrics, and compliance activities to support leadership decision-making.


Partnerships and Market Engagement


  • Work directly with trading firms, banks, commodity producers, data vendors, and fintechs to understand data consumption needs.
  • Identify and form new partnerships aligned with Abaxx data strategy.
  • Support sales and relationship teams with market data product positioning and client onboarding
  • Act as a subject matter expert for clients on market data structure, delivery, and licensing
  • Built and maintained strong, long-term relationships with partners, ensuring clarity of expectations, obligations, and value delivery.
  • Develop partner materials (program overviews, capability summaries, use-case frameworks) to support outreach and engagement.

Required Qualifications & Experience

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Finance, Economics, or related field
  • 5-10 years of experience in market data strategy, product management, or data infrastructure within:
  • Derivatives or securities exchanges
  • Market data vendors or financial data providers
  • Trading venues, clearing houses, or fintech platforms
  • Strong understanding of commodities derivatives markets and exchange data flows

ad.title: Market Research & Product Development Analyst
ad.description:
About the job


Market Research & Product Development Analyst (Singapore / London)


We are seeking a motivated and analytically strong professional to join our Product Development team as a Commodity Derivatives Market Research & Product Development Analyst.

This role supports the research of relevant commodity markets as well as interacting with the design, and enhancement of Abaxx derivative contracts with the goal to create market relevant products to maximize robust price discovery and liquidity opportunities.


Key Responsibilities

  • Conduct ongoing research into global commodity markets, with focus on Precious Metals, Battery Materials, LNG, Power and environmental products.
  • Research physical and Listed/OTC derivatives markets with focus on supply-demand fundamentals, seasonality, and macroeconomic drivers to offer product development opportunities and empower the commercial team.
  • Monitor competitive exchange offerings and global market developments to identify product gaps and innovation opportunities.
  • Engage with market participants across client segments to gather market feedback and insights.
  • Assist in drafting and reviewing contract specifications, including delivery terms, settlement methodology, contract size, tick value, and expiry structure with full trade life cycle perspective.
  • Contribute to market supervisory activities in coordination with risk and regulatory teams.
  • Support the development of analytical reports, market commentary, and internal dashboards for product, sales, and strategy teams with the potential for external client and/or event presentations, if desired.


Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor's degree in finance, economics, mathematics, engineering, statistics, or a related field.
  • 5+ years of relevant experience in commodity markets, price reporting derivatives analysis, exchange operations, product management, or market research.
  • Solid understanding of exchange-traded derivatives and commodity market pricing.
  • Familiarity with exchange rulebooks, contract specifications, and product approval processes.
  • Understanding of regulatory frameworks applicable to derivatives markets (e.g., SFA, CEA, EMIR, , MiFID II).
  • Experience using market data platforms such as Bloomberg, Refinitiv, and/or exchange analytics tools.
  • Strong analytical skills with proficiency in Excel and at least one analytical or programming tool (e.g., Python, R, SQL).
  • Ability to communicate technical concepts clearly to internal and external stakeholders.

ad.title: Exchange and Clearing Operations Analyst
ad.description:
About Abaxx Abaxx is a full-stack financial technology company developing a regulated commodity futures exchange and clearinghouse in Singapore, and new software protocols and applications to enhance trust in electronic markets.
Abaxx Exchange was founded on the principle of developing SmarterMarkets™ that are characterized by more efficient, seamless, and secure transactions. Our goal is to improve price signals and better risk management tools for energy markets to accelerate the energy transition. Fundamental to the development of our societies, we believe that commodities can and should be graded and traded based on their environmental, social, and governance (ESG) externalities. We have been granted approval in principle by MAS as a Recognized Market Operator (RMO) and Approved Clearing House (ACH). We are a majority-owned subsidiary of Abaxx Technologies, a publicly traded financial technology company listed on the NEO Exchange in Canada (www.abaxx.tech).
About the role We are thrilled to welcome aspired and driven fresh graduates to join our team as an Exchange and Clearing Operations Analyst. In this role, you will be an integral part of our exchange and clearing house, contributing to the seamless execution of trading activities. Your attention to detail, analytical mindset, and readiness for shift duties will be key assets as you embark on this exciting journey in the financial industry.
Responsibilities


  • Central Function: Serve at the core of the exchange and clearing business by ensuring seamless execution, stability, and integrity across all operational processes to deliver the organization’s objectives and operational outcomes effectively.
  • Precision in Trade Execution: Gain hands-on experience in executing and meticulously monitoring trade, clearing and settlement processes, ensuring accuracy and efficiency.
  • Regulatory Adherence: Develop a comprehensive grasp of Exchange and Clearing Rules and Procedures. Play an active role in upholding compliance with exchange and clearing regulations.
  • Insightful Data Analysis: Utilize your keen analytical skills and financial acumen to support the evaluation of trading activities, market trends to facilitate the generation of informed reports.
  • Holistic Team Collaboration: Engage closely with diverse teams and external partners to ensure the harmonious functioning of exchange and clearing operations.
  • SOP Compliance: Develop a thorough understanding of Standard Operating Procedures and ensure consistent adherence to established workflows and controls.
  • Reporting Excellence: Uphold accuracy, timeliness, and consistency in all operational and regulatory reporting processes.
  • Innovative Process Refinement: Identify avenues to optimize operational processes, infusing your fresh perspective for the continuous enhancement of efficiency.
  • Quality Customer Service: Assist and interact with market participants, addressing their inquiries, resolving issues.
  • Operational continuity: This role requires working on a rotating shift schedule to maintain consistent coverage and support for 22/5 exchange and clearing operations. Shift allowance will be provided for eligible shift duties.



Qualifications


  • Recent Bachelor's degree in Finance, Economics, or a related field.
  • Passion for the financial industry and a determined aspiration to excel in exchange and clearing operations.
  • Solid grasp of financial instruments, combined with an eagerness to learn about trading mechanisms and clearing processes.
  • Strong analytical skills, enabling quick comprehension of complex concepts and data.
  • Proactive approach to understanding and adhering to regulatory compliance within financial markets.
  • Effective communication skills and a collaborative mindset, essential for team-based operations.
  • Ability to thrive in a shift-based work environment, ensuring seamless support around the clock.

Benefits

  • Competitive entry-level salary with growth potential.
  • Comprehensive training and mentorship from experienced professionals.
  • Exposure to a dynamic and supportive work environment.
  • Opportunities for career development and advancement.

ad.title: Risk Analyst (Welcome Fresh Graduates)
ad.description:
Abaxx Exchange is looking for a Risk Analyst who will assist in overseeing and shaping the financial risk management practice in Singapore. The candidate will join a dynamic team, be exposed to a wide range of products and be challenged with complex risk problems. This is an exciting, multifaceted role where you will interact with various teams to broaden your exposure.

 

Responsibilities:


Risk Control

  • Assist with the establishment and maintenance of the risk management framework by adhering to internal risk policies and staying up to date with regulatory standards and industry best practices
  • Execute daily risk control processes including initial and variation margin calculations, mark-to-market valuations, and risk reports, working alongside Clearing Operation teams to manage clearing risk
  • Investigate and document margin breaches and risk limit excesses, including root cause analysis, member communication, escalation procedures, and regulatory reporting requirements. Maintain detailed audit trails and contribute to post-incident reviews to strengthen risk controls
  • Support the calculation and monitoring of option margin requirements using portfolio margining methodologies (e.g., SPAN, VaR-based approaches), including reviewing volatility surface parameters, stress scenarios, and option Greeks for complex positions
  • Assist in the administration of non-cash collateral including government securities, letters of credit, and emerging digital assets. Support the evaluation of collateral valuation methodologies, haircut policies, and operational processes including digital title transfer mechanisms for tokenized assets
  • Monitor collateral adequacy and concentration limits across clearing members, including daily reconciliation of posted collateral against margin requirements and guarantee fund contributions
  • Build and maintain reports for regular management, member and regulatory reporting
  • Monitor market trends, geopolitical events that may impact products to proactively identify emerging risk and opportunities
  • Develop and maintain close working relationships with the Clearing & Exchange Operations, Compliance and Finance teams
  • Compile and provide information to external parties (e.g. regulators, auditors, external independent validators, consultants) on routine and ad-hoc bases
  • Participate in new product launches, new member onboarding, regular clearing member reviews and run thematic or ad-hoc market research on relevant risk topics

 

Risk Development:

  • Contribute to development and enhancement of in-house risk application leveraging Python, SQL, GIT and AWS cloud infrastructure to support advanced analytics and risk management process.
  • Monitor the performance of risk models, ensuring their appropriateness and maintaining the integrity and accuracy of risk data.
  • Participate in stress testing exercises to evaluate margin adequacy under extreme market scenarios, including reverse stress testing and sensitivity analysis for key risk parameters
  • Develop and leverage upon advanced analytics tools and models, e.g. tools and models related to Artificial Intelligence (AI)
  • Design test cases and perform testing for risk systems development and system upgrades
  • Contribute to the development and successful delivery of visual analytics initiatives. These initiatives typically include the development of reports, analysis, data visualizations and dashboards from aggregated data sources such as risk databases with the objective of bringing risk monitoring, analysis and strategic decision making to the next level.

 

Risk Culture:

  • Contribute to the establishment and promotion of risk culture within the Company including through daily responsibilities, awareness efforts and training efforts
  • Contribute to the culture of governance and risk oversight within the Company







Qualifications:

  • Degree in Risk Management, Business, Economics, Mathematics, Science, Engineering or IT, or a related discipline
  • Working level competency in Excel VBA/SQL/Python and Microsoft Office applications
  • Understanding of derivatives products including futures and options, margin methodologies, and collateral management principles preferred
  • Strong attention to detail with a focus on process execution
  • Keen interest in financial, commodity and energy markets and derivative instruments
  • Key player in an agile and diverse environment that emphasizes proactive collaboration and risk identification
  • Quick learner with excellent written and communication skills, team orientation, and ability to work with counterparts in different organizational areas
  • Self-starter who takes initiative to improve the current state of things and is adaptable to change
  • Prior relevant professional experience in risk management, data analysis, portfolio management, and/or risk systems in prime brokerages, fund houses or clearing houses preferred

ad.title: Business Intelligence & Data Analyst (Graduate)
ad.description:
We are looking for a motivated Graduate Business Intelligence & Data Analyst to join our team based in Singapore. 

This is an entry-level role designed for recent graduates who are interested in financial markets, data analysis and workflow automation 

You will work closely with a commercially driven team across Strategy, Sales and Product development to build relevant data sets and market analyses to empower stakeholders for both branding and revenue generation.  


Key Responsibilities 

  • Perform data analysis to support reporting, operational insights, and decision-making via dashboard and visual presentations to various internal stakeholders. 
  • Support automated data collection from internal systems and external data sources whilst keeping a mindset of continued data optimization and reducing manual processes. 
  • Build and analyse bespoke data sets alongside the product development team and take part in project related analyses with the business development and strategy functions. 
  • Assist in gathering and documenting business and data requirements under the guidance of senior stakeholders. Help map business processes and data flows from various sources to help grow data sets as per required. 
  • Assist with testing and validating data pipelines and automated workflows 
  • Collaborate with technology teams, data engineers, and business users on assigned initiatives 
  • Build an understanding of derivatives markets and exchange operations with the potential of external interactions if desired. 
  • Follow established data governance and security standards 


Required Skills & Qualifications 

  • Bachelor’s degree (or expected completion) in Finance, Economics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, or a related discipline 
  • Basic understanding of derivatives and commodity markets and basic understanding of fundamentals of price correlations and asset prices. 
  • Understanding of data concepts (e.g., databases, datasets, data quality) 
  • Exposure to SQL, Python, R, or similar tools through coursework or internships 
  • Familiarity with Excel, data visualization, or reporting tools 
  • Good written and verbal communication skills with an entrepreneurial and collaborative mindset. 
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills

ad.title: Market Development Manager (Relationships)
ad.description:
We are seeking an experienced Relationship Manager to onboard, manage and grow relationships with participants of our Market Development Program across the exchange’s commodities derivatives markets.  

This role is critical to managing client interactions while monitoring performance versus program requirements. 

The successful candidate will have a strong understanding of exchange trading mechanics, market making strategies, and liquidity incentive structures, and will work closely with internal Product, Market Operations, Technology and Commercial teams to optimize participant performance and ultimately program returns to effectively deepen liquidity of Abaxx markets.  


Key Responsibilities 

  • Act as the primary relationship manager for designated Market Maker and Liquidity Development participants 
  • Build trusted, long-term partnerships with trading firms, proprietary trading houses, banks, and specialist market makers 
  • Gain a thorough understanding of Abaxx Market Development Program structure, client trading strategies, risk appetites, operational requirements, including infrastructure set up 
  • Work closely with Product Leads, CMO and Legal to launch new programs and revise existing programs to continuously deepen market liquidity while meeting budget constraints and business objectives  
  • Manage the rollout of any new program revisions, which includes socialising the new program parameters with participants before revising the program rules with legal, and then formally rolling out the new program on an agreed date 
  • Monitor market maker performance against program requirements (eg. Max Spread, Min Quantity, Min Volume thresholds etc) 
  • Actively seek market feedbackto optimize participation and ensure alignment between client objectives and exchange market quality goals 
  • In exceptional market conditions, collaborate with internal stakeholders to temporarily adjust program parameters eg. widen spreads, following consultation with market participants, to ensure continuous liquidity  
  • Work with internal (Market Operations, Product, Technology, Legal, Risk) and external stakeholders (eg. Technology vendors or CMs) to resolve any client issues  
  • Develop a deep understanding of all product contract chapters, exchange and clearing rules, order types, matching logic, and risk framework and settlement cycles 
  • Provide feedback to internal teams on market structure, contract design, and incentive effectiveness 
  • Identify opportunities to expand market maker participation across additional products or markets 
  • Support new product launches by securing and onboarding market development participants  
  • Coordinate onboarding, certification, and technical readiness with Market Regulation, Legal, Market Operations, Technology for market development participants and work with market development team to track enrolment. 
  • Understand the BAU workflow of the Market Development team and be able to utilise the various applications used for enrolment tracking, performance tracking and payout qualification 
  • Recommend infrastructure enhancements that can be justified to support deeper liquidity and improve performance goals 
  • Support rule changes, incentive revisions, and trading enhancements impacting market makers 
  • Prepare regular reports on market maker performance, incentive utilization, and liquidity outcomes 
  • Provide insights and recommendations to senior management on liquidity strategy and client engagement 


Required Qualifications & Experience 

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Business, Mathematics, or a related field 
  • 2 to 5 years of experience in a client-facing or trading-related role within: 
    • Commodities or financial derivatives exchanges 
    • Proprietary trading firms or market makers 
    • Investment banks or trading venues 
  • Strong understanding of exchange-traded derivatives and market making dynamics 
  • Solid knowledge of exchange trading workflows, order books, and market structure 
  • Strong commercial and relationship management skills 
  • Analytical mindset with the ability to interpret trading and liquidity data 
  • Excellent communication and negotiation skills 
  • Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced, market-driven environment 
  • Direct experience managing or participating in exchange market making programs 
  • Familiarity with incentive design and liquidity

ad.title: Market & Client Support
ad.description:
We are seeking a driven, detail-oriented and curious individual to join our team.  The Market and Client Support role supports clients and internal stakeholders across client onboarding, due diligence, and ongoing relationship management. The role acts as a key liaison between Commercial, Market Operations, Risk, Technology, and other internal teams to ensure client issues are resolved efficiently, client and vendor relationships are well supported, and market support documentation are maintained in line with the exchange and clearing framework, rules, procedures, and internal policies.


Key Responsibilities




Client Onboarding, Due Diligence & Relationship Support

  • Act as the primary point of contact for external clients during the onboarding process, coordinating communication and progress across internal teams such as Market Operations, Technology, Risk, Regulatory and Commercial teams
  • Support client onboarding and due diligence processes by providing required documentation, information, and clarifications
  • Ensure onboarding timelines are met
  • Act as a coordination point to help investigate, manage, and rectify client issues during onboarding and ongoing relationship support, working with relevant internal teams as required
  • Assist with ongoing relationship management by preparing relevant documentation, updates, and responses to sales- and market-related queries

 

Exchange & Clearing Framework Knowledge

  • Maintain a strong working understanding of the exchange and clearing framework
  • Be familiar with applicable rules, procedures, and internal policies and understand how they impact clients
  • Support internal and external queries relating to commercial and client-facing aspects of the framework


Technology Vendor Coordination & Management

  • Act as a day-to-day coordination point for technology vendors in relation to client onboarding, market access, and ongoing client support
  • Coordinate vendor inputs, timelines, and information required to support client onboarding and issue resolution
  • Track vendor-related issues, actions, and dependencies, escalating where required to internal Technology or Operations teams


Documentation & Information Management

  • Prepare, maintain, and revise key client-facing documentation, including:
    • Client guides, onboarding materials, and FAQs
    • Internal process and SOP documents and templates
  • Ensure documentation is accurate, current, and aligned with approved frameworks and policies


Onboarding Progress Tracking & Reporting

  • Track and maintain accurate records of client onboarding status, milestones, and dependencies
  • Prepare regular onboarding progress updates for internal stakeholders, including Commercial, while maintaining audit-ready onboarding records and documentation via internal CRM.
  • Support visibility of onboarding timelines, risks, and blockers, escalating issues where appropriate


Business Infrastructure & Process Support

  • Support the build-out and ongoing maintenance of departmental business infrastructure, including:
    • Standardised templates and document repositories
    • Trackers for projects, onboarding, approvals, client issues, and vendor interactions
    • Defined workflows for client support processes
  • Identify gaps, inefficiencies, or risks in existing processes and propose improvements

 

Required Qualifications & Experience

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Computer Science, Economics, Business, Law, or a related field.
  • 3–7 years of experience in client onboarding, client services, operations, or compliance within a relevant business such as a derivatives exchange, clearing house, broker, bank or technology vendor.
  • Understanding of commodities derivatives markets and how market participants interact with full trade life cycle of derivatives.
  • Solid knowledge of KYC, AML, and regulatory onboarding requirements
  • Project management or process improvement experience.

ad.title: Commodity Sales - India & Middle East Focus
ad.description:
The Commodity Sales professional will be responsible for driving client acquisition, trading volume, and revenue growth for the exchange’s listed commodity derivatives across Indian related Institutional business, promoting the exchange’s products and market structure, and acting as the key interface between onshore/offshore clients based in Singapore, India and the Middle East and the exchange’s global platform. 

 

Key Responsibilities 

  • Execute a focused sales strategy for institutional trading firms to grow trading volumes and open interest in listed commodity derivatives in a target driven environment. 
  • Identify, onboard, and maintain strong relationships with cross segment Indian firms trading from Singapore, India and Dubai. 
  • Conduct client visits, roadshows, and product workshops as required. 
  • Analyse client activity, market share, and competitive landscape to identify growth opportunities. 
  • Understand client hedging, trading, and investment needs and propose solutions using listed derivatives. Collect and distribute feedback internally via HubSpot platform and presentation regular business updates to management 
  • Educate clients on cross border market access channels, trading and clearing arrangements, and the use of offshore contracts to complement onshore risk management. 
  • Contribute to marketing campaigns, webinars, roadshows, and industry events to raise the profile of the exchange’s commodity franchise. 
  • Work closely with Onboarding teams to ensure a seamless client experience from onboarding through live trading. 
  • Prepare regular business reviews, market intelligence reports, and strategy updates for regional and global management. 

 

Qualifications & Experience 

  • Bachelor’s degree in finance, Economics, Business, Engineering, or related field; advanced degree or professional qualification (e.g., CFA, FRM) is an advantage. 
  • 5–10+ years of experience in commodity derivatives sales or closely related roles at an exchange, broker, bank, FCM, or trading house. 
  • Strong understanding of derivatives, margining, and clearing, with specific experience in OTC to futures trading with physical related clients. 
  • Demonstrated track record of building and managing institutional or corporate client relationships and achieving commercial targets. 
  • Familiarity with exchange trading platforms, market data, and typical front-to-back workflows in derivatives markets. 

 

Skills & Competencies 

  • Entrepreneurial spirit:  Willing to own and manage a set of clients and feed ideas back into the collective team structure for potential synergies with colleagues. 
  • Commercial Mindset: Strong sales instincts, negotiation skills, and ability to identify and convert opportunities. 
  • Technical Knowledge: Solid grasp of commodity market fundamentals, derivatives pricing basics, hedging strategies, and risk management use cases. 
  • Client Focus: Ability to understand client business models, articulate value propositions, and provide solution-oriented advice. 
  • Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills; confident in presenting to senior stakeholders and at industry events. 
  • Analytical Ability: Comfortable using data and analytics to track performance, identify trends, and support commercial decisions. 
  • Collaboration: Effective team player, able to work across product, technology, risk, clearing, and operations. 
  • Regulatory Awareness: Understanding of the regulatory environment for derivatives trading and clearing in relevant jurisdictions.

ad.title: Business Analyst – Business Intelligence, Data Collection & Automation
ad.description:
We are seeking an experienced Business Analyst with a strong focus on data collection, process automation, and analytics to support the operation and development of a commercial data hub and business intelligence unit. This role will sit at the intersection of business development, Sales and technology to build relevant data sets to assist in the growth of the business.  

The successful candidate will play a key role in building this new function to identify data requirements, optimizing data flows, and driving automation initiatives that improve efficiency, accuracy, and scalability to empower the Sales, Product development and Marketing teams across exchange products. 


Key Responsibilities 

  • Translate business requirements into functional and technical specifications for execution and ongoing optimization while creating functional output for business analyses. 
  • Lead and Define data models, schemas, data quality rules and various collection sources in collaboration with engineering teams and development cycles. 
  • Identify opportunities to automate manual or, if required, semi-manual operational workflows. 
  • Create dashboards and reports to highlight relevant data points for internal sales and marketing teams whilst assisting stakeholders in building data driven presentations for both internal and external review.   
  • Build and analyse bespoke data sets alongside the product development team and take part in project related analyses with the business development and strategy functions. 
  • Create procedures for daily maintenance with the view on ongoing development pipelines in conjunction with technology teams. Act as a bridge between business users and technology teams. 
  • Champion the teams’ goals, educate and empower business teams with relevant and current data trends to create a collaborative and entrepreneurial environment


Required Skills & Experience 

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, Mathematics, Finance, or a related discipline 
  • 1–3 years’ experience in a technical business analyst, data analyst, or similar role (or exceptional graduate experience) 
  • Understanding of financial and commodity markets with emphasis on price correlation fundamentals. 
  • Understanding of Derivatives products fundamentals including market data, trade lifecycle, margining, and risk management. 
  • Strong SQL skills and experience working with relational and analytical databases 
  • Solid understanding of data pipelines, ETL/ELT processes, and automation concepts 
  • Ability to read and understand technical documentation, APIs, and system designs 
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills

ad.title: Commercial & Operations Support Analyst
ad.description:
Overview

We are in the precious metals business, launching with a vision to innovate and excel in the industry. If you are a motivated and proactive individual, join us on this exciting journey and contribute to shaping our future. This role will primarily focus on providing support on business development initiatives, client onboarding, as well as supporting day-to-day business operations. This is an excellent opportunity for a individual with an entrepreneurial mindset to take ownership and make an impact in a new venture.


Key Responsibilities

  • Provide support on business development initiatives  to expand precious metals business, including the addition of new products and services.
  • Manage end‑to‑end client onboarding, serving as the primary point of contact and coordinating internally to resolve any blockers or issues.
  • Engage with clients on pricing and market updates, supporting efforts to attract trading volume and strengthen client relationships in collaboration with the Managing Director and Sales team.
  • Develop and prepare compelling pitch decks and comprehensive documentation for external clients, partners, and internal leadership.
  • Operations support:
    • Assist in executing daily operational tasks, including trade processing, inventory tracking, and customer inquiries as the business ramps up.
    • Support system testing and troubleshooting to ensure smooth functionality of operational tools and platforms.
  • Take on additional tasks as needed, such as data entry, website updates, research, or assisting with marketing initiatives.
  • Proactively identify areas for improvement and suggest solutions to enhance efficiency and scalability.


Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor's degree holder
  • Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.
  • An entrepreneurial mindset with a proven ability to take initiative and responsibility for tasks.
  • A resourceful and adaptable approach, thriving in a startup environment with a passion for problem-solving.
  • Possess a can-do attitude, eagerness to learn and take up new challenges.
  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a small, fast-moving team.
  • Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal.



Preferred Qualifications (Not required but good to have)


  • 1 to 2 years of working experience involved in commodities or related roles, preferably within physical and precious metals.
  • Basic coding skills (e.g., HTML, CSS, Python, or similar) to assist with website or system-related tasks.
  • Interest or familiarity with the precious metals industry (training will be provided).
  • Background in driving business development and supporting new initiatives
  • Experience with process documentation or operational workflows.
  • Availability to start immediately preferred

ad.title: Duty Manager (Islandwide)
ad.description:
Job Expectations

  • Responsible for ensuring the proper, efficient, and profitable functioning of the Hotel.
  • Supervise sections in the Front Office, including reception, cashier, telephone, reservation, and baggage services. Monitor staff members’ conduct and job performance and to project a positive corporate image to guests.
  • Provide prompt, courteous, and efficient service to guests and handle guest complaints with tact and diplomacy. Assist in guest check-ins and check-outs.
  • Assist in inspecting rooms assigned to VIPs before their arrival, escort VIPs to their rooms and ensure complimentary amenities are provided. Inform relevant departments about VIPs’ arrival.
  • Check the Logbook for messages and follow up actions at the beginning of each shift.  Issue keys to authorized personnel only and initiate correspondence regarding enquiries, reservations, and complaints. Monitor housekeeping discrepancy reports and take corrective actions when necessary.
  • Handle security-related matters, such as directing guests reporting incidents or theft and addressing guest conduct issues with the Security Department.
  • Maintain the cash float amount according to expected occupancy. Authorize rate and room changes, paid outs, cash advances, and acceptance of cheques following procedures and credit policies.
  • Take charge of training all reception staff members, include planning, organizing and conducting on-the-job training.
  • Conduct spot checks on outlets in the absence of the Outlet Manager.
  • Monitor room inventory closely to maximize room utilization and generate higher revenue. Collaborate with Sales to optimize revenue and occupancy from group allotments.
  • Take on the responsibility of evacuating staff members and guests during a fire in the absence of the Safety & Fire Manager. Attend CERT training as required by law and regulations.
  • Perform any other duties assigned by supervisors, carrying them out diligently and professionally.

Requirements

  • Minimum Diploma in Hospitality, Tourism, or related field.
  • At least 2–3 years of Front Office or supervisory experience in the hotel industry.
  • Proficient in Opera PMS or other hotel management systems.
  • Hospitality certifications (e.g., CERT, service excellence, leadership, or operations) are an advantage.
  • Strong leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills.
  • Service-oriented with the ability to handle guest feedback and operational issues effectively.
  • Well-groomed and professional in appearance and demeanor.
  • Willing to work rotating shifts, weekends, and public holidays.

 

Locations Available: 

  • The Barracks Hotel Sentosa
  • The Outpost Hotel Sentosa
  • Oasia Resort Sentosa
  • Village Hotel Sentosa

ad.title: Assistant Manager, Email and Loyalty Marketing
ad.description:
We are looking for a hands-on and analytical marketer to support and grow our membership and loyalty ecosystem. This role is responsible for executing lifecycle strategies, improving member engagement, and contributing to the commercial performance of the loyalty programme. You will work closely with senior stakeholders across marketing, revenue, operations and finance to strengthen member acquisition, engagement and retention through CRM and marketing automation.

Loyalty Program Management

  • Support the development and execution of the loyalty program roadmaps to drive member growth and repeat stays
  • Monitor programme KPIs including member acquisition, activation, retention, tier progression and revenue contribution.
  • Manage day-to-day operations of programs including member enrolment flows, benefits configuration and campaign setup.
  • Track points accrual and redemption trends, and support liability monitoring and breakage analysis in partnership with Finance.
  • Assist in evaluating programme enhancements based on performance data and competitor benchmarking.

CRM & Lifecycle Marketing

  • Build and optimize automated lifecycle journeys across onboarding, pre-stay, post-stay, win-back and tier upgrade triggers.
  • Execute email and CRM campaigns including segmentation, personalization and dynamic content deployment.
  • Conduct A/B testing on subject lines, creative, offers and journey logic to improve engagement and conversion.
  • Monitor email deliverability, database health and compliance with data regulations.

Campaign Execution & Member Growth

  • Plan and execute loyalty-led campaigns to drive member acquisition, points earning and redemption.
  • Collaborate with brand, digital marketing and property teams to ensure loyalty messaging is integrated into broader campaigns.
  • Support tactical promotions that stimulate member activity while maintaining commercial discipline.

Analytics & Reporting

  • Track and report regular insights on member behaviour, revenue contribution and campaign performance using CRM dashboards, Google Analytics and business intelligence tools.
  • Maintain dashboards that track loyalty KPIs and campaign ROI.

Governance & Stakeholder Support

  • Maintain CRM data accuracy through routine audits and quality checks.
  • Develop and update SOPs and any documentation related to loyalty programme and emai marketing operations.
  • Support onboarding and training of property teams on loyalty processes and systems.
  • Liaise with CRM vendors or internal tech teams on basic troubleshooting and system enhancements.
  • Perform any other duties assigned by supervisor, carrying them out diligently and professionally. 
Requirements
  • Degree in Business, Marketing, Hospitality, or a related field.
  • Minimum 4-6 years of work experience in loyalty programme management and marketing
  • Hands-on experience with both CRM systems and marketing automation systems
  • Detail-oriented with good operational discipline
  • Strong copywriting and analytical skills
  • Comfortable working cross-functionally and managing multiple campaigns simultaneously

ad.title: Engineering Supervisor
ad.description:
About Us

Far East Organization is a Christian Enterprise, which develops real estate and operates businesses by serving with grace, love, integrity and honesty. Together with its Hong Kong-based sister company Sino Group, they are one of Asia’s largest real estate groups, with operations in Singapore, Malaysia, Australia, Japan, China (Mainland and Hong Kong) and USA. 

 

Far East Organization is the largest private property developer in Singapore, having developed over 780 developments across all segments of real estate including 55,000 private homes in Singapore since its establishment in 1960. Far East Organization includes three listed entities: Far East Orchard Limited, Far East Hospitality Trust and Yeo Hiap Seng Limited. 

 

Far East Organization is the winner of 14 FIABCI World Prix d’Excellence awards, the highest honour in international real estate. In 2022, Far East Organization is listed as top 50 of The Straits Time’s Singapore Best Employers Study. We believe in taking care of the total well-being of our people as they grow with us. We nurture a community where talent is developed and recognised, innovative ideas are encouraged, and achievements celebrated. 

 

Our health and well-being benefits provide physical, mental and spiritual support to our people and their families. In addition to annual leave, performance bonus, medical and insurance coverage, you can anticipate the provision of birthday leave, birthday benefits, care leave, flexible benefits, staff rebates at F&B outlets, and special rates at our hotels, both locally and overseas. We empower you in your engagement with the wider community by providing varied activities to experience different opportunities to serve and contribute to the community.

Responsibilities

  • Conduct briefings to technicians before start of shift and ensure proper handing over and taking over of shifts.
  • Attend daily morning briefing and represent CE for site meeting in the absence of Engineering Executive.
  • Attend to Tenants and customer feedback and complaints for areas under landlord care.
  • Schedule daily checks on all common areas, toilets, public walkways, external façade and M&E facilities and its plant room. Follow up on outstanding defects.
  • Responsible for the preventive maintenance of the equipment in accordance with Preventive Maintenance Schedule and ensure that the records are kept and up to date for periodic review.
  • Coordinate with Housekeeping on schedule of CARE rooms.
  • Respond to M&E-related emergencies at property.
  • Escalation of incident or accident at site.
  • Supervise technician’s work.

Requirements

  • Degree in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering, or equivalent
  • At least 2 years of relevant experience in retail malls, hotels, serviced residences or residential properties
  • Strong leadership, good planning, organisational, communication and interpersonal skills
  • Knowledge of chiller system and M&E services in commercial buildings

ad.title: Assistant Manager, Treasury (Capital Management)
ad.description:
About Us


Far East Organization is a Christian Enterprise, which develops real estate and operates businesses by serving with grace, love, integrity and honesty. Together with its Hong Kong-based sister company Sino Group, they are one of Asia’s largest real estate groups, with operations in Singapore, Malaysia, Australia, Japan, China (Mainland and Hong Kong) and USA. 

Far East Organization is the largest private property developer in Singapore, having developed over 780 developments across all segments of real estate including 55,000 private homes in Singapore since its establishment in 1960. Far East Organization includes three listed entities: Far East Orchard Limited, Far East Hospitality Trust and Yeo Hiap Seng Limited.




Far East Organization is the winner of 14 FIABCI World Prix d’Excellence awards, the highest honour in international real estate. In 2022, Far East Organization is listed as top 50 of The Straits Time’s Singapore Best Employers Study. We believe in taking care of the total well-being of our people as they grow with us. We nurture a community where talent is developed and recognised, innovative ideas are encouraged, and achievements celebrated.

Our health and well-being benefits provide physical, mental and spiritual support to our people and their families. In addition to annual leave, performance bonus, medical and insurance coverage, you can anticipate the provision of birthday leave, birthday benefits, care leave, flexible benefits, staff rebates at F&B outlets, and special rates at our hotels, both locally and overseas. We empower you in your engagement with the wider community by providing varied activities to experience different opportunities to serve and contribute to the community.


Responsibilities

  • Assist in the monitoring and management of the Group's cash position, liquidity planning and deployment of surplus funds.
  • Manage the Group's bank accounts.
  • Collaborate with Tax Department on the repatriation of excess cash from overseas investments.
  • Prepare regular cash flow forecasts and reports for Management.
  • Support the Team Lead and Head of Treasury in the capital management for new projects and investments.
  • Develop, review and update Treasury policies, processes and controls relating to cash and capital management.
  • Support in administering, troubleshooting and implementing enhancements for the Group’s Treasury Management System.
  • Implement approved hedging plans to minimise the foreign currency exposure.
  • Keep abreast of the forex market movements and perform research/analysis on economic and market trends/outlook.

Requirements

  • Degree in Accountancy, Finance or equivalent qualification.
  • Minimum 5 years of relevant experience in cash and capital management.
  • Detail-oriented, independent and analytical with good problem solving skills.
  • Strong communication, organisational and time management skills.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office.
  • Prior experience with Treasury Management System is required.
  • Knowledge of Bloomberg will be advantageous.

ad.title: Leasing Executive
ad.description:
Responsibilities
  • Identify and prospect for potential tenants through market research, networking and outreach
  • Negotiate lease terms for new leases and renewals 
  • Finalise lease agreements and other leasing-related documentation
  • Conduct competitive market research and analysis to understand current trends in the real estate industry
  • Liaise with tenants and building occupants on leasing and tenancy related matters
Requirements:
  • Diploma in Real Estate, Business or any other relevant disciplines. Candidates with RES License preferred.
  • Preferably with 3 years of experience in retail/commercial leasing
  • Good negotiation and communication skills
  • Ability to build rapport with diverse stakeholders

ad.title: IT Infrastructure Specialist/Lead
ad.description:
Infrastructure Operations & Service Stability

  • Support day-to-day operations across corporate infrastructure platforms including:
    • Network (LAN/WAN/Wi-Fi), Internet connectivity, segmentation
    • Servers/virtualization (on-premise and cloud)
    • Identity & access (AD/Entra ID), DNS/DHCP
    • Email & collaboration platforms (Microsoft 365)
    • Backup, recovery, storage and monitoring
  • Troubleshoot and resolve escalated infrastructure incidents with internal teams and vendors.
  • Perform system health checks and service reviews, driving continuous reliability improvements.
  • Maintain operational documentation such as runbooks, escalation procedures, and system references.

 

Cybersecurity, Governance & Resilience

  • Support infrastructure security controls including: endpoint posture, firewall/access controls, vulnerability remediation, security logging/monitoring, and privileged access practices.
  • Contribute to patching, access reviews, change governance, and post-incident corrective actions.
  • Support backup operations, recovery testing, and disaster recovery readiness.

 

Vendor / MSP Oversight & Project Support

  • Coordinate with vendors and managed service providers (MSPs) to ensure quality delivery and timely follow-ups.
  • Support technical inputs for procurement, evaluation, and service acceptance activities.
  • Assist infrastructure solutioning and delivery support for corporate enhancements and technology refresh projects.

Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related discipline.
  • 3–7 years of relevant experience in infrastructure operations, implementation, and/or support (level dependent).
  • Strong working knowledge in: networking, servers, cloud, identity, Microsoft 365, backup/DR, monitoring, and security controls.
  • Familiarity with Microsoft Azure and infrastructure security practices is preferred.
  • Certifications are a plus: CCNA, CompTIA Network+/Security+, ITIL Foundation, Microsoft Azure certifications, security certifications (CCSP/GSEC).
  • Strong documentation, problem-solving, and stakeholder communication skills.

ad.title: Class 4 Delivery Driver
ad.description:
About Us

Agape Services is a Christian social enterprise under Far East Organization, whose aim is to provide meaningful employment and benefits to people with disadvantaged background, as a demonstration of the love and teachings of Jesus Christ.

 

Agape Services offer services in Agape Cleaning, Agape Laundry as well as moving and disposal. We aim to provide employment through our services to the disadvantaged who would otherwise not be able to cope in a regular work environment.


Responsibilities:

- To perform daily collection and delivery of Central Laundry items

- To maintain the general cleanliness of the vehicle which shall include car washing

- To carry out any other duties as assigned by the management or its representative from time to time


Requirements:

- Possess Class 4 Driving License, with good driving records

- Familiar with major roads in Singapore

- Good communication skills

- Able to carry heavy laundry loads

 

Recieve a $500 Sign-on Reward!

Stay with us for 12 months to qualify. Terms and conditions apply.

ad.title: Marketing Manager (Retail)
ad.description:
Responsibilities:
  • Plan and execute A&P initiatives for assigned commercial properties to boost footfall, drive shopper traffic, and increase spending.
  • Collaborate with tenants to encourage active participation in A&P programs and gather feedback for continuous improvement.
  • Build and maintain strategic partnerships with sponsors to enhance the reach and appeal of property programs.
  • Implement and oversee loyalty programs in line with group policies, ensuring effective CRM operations and member engagement throughout the customer journey.
  • Prepare annual budgets and forecasts, deliver management reports, and conduct competitor analysis to inform strategy.
  • Launch, monitor, and optimize campaigns across platforms such as Google, Facebook, and Instagram to maximize ROI.
  • Develop and execute a comprehensive marketing calendar to maintain ongoing engagement with loyalty members.
  • Manage improvement projects for the CRM mobile application and website to elevate user experience.
  • Identify new growth opportunities by leveraging insights from customer and campaign data.
Requirements:
  • Degree in Marketing, Mass Communications or any other equivalent discipline
  • Minimum 6 years of experience in brand marketing of commercial properties
  • Excellent communication, interpersonal and leadership skills
  • Results-oriented and able to meet tight deadlines
  • Creative and resourceful

ad.title: Leasing Manager (Retail)
ad.description:
Responsibilities:

As Leasing Manager, you will lead and support the leasing initiatives by executing leasing plans, mentoring team members, while developing and maintaining a robust network of potential tenants to secure new leasing opportunities.

• Identify and prospect for potential tenants through market research, networking and outreach

• Negotiate lease terms for new leases and renewals

• Finalise lease agreements and other leasing-related documentation

• Conduct competitive market research and analysis to understand current trends in the real estate industry

• Liaise with tenants and building occupants on leasing and tenancy related matters    

Requirements:
​

• Degree in Real Estate, Business or any other relevant disciplines, with minimum 5 years' experience in retail leasing. Candidates with RES License preferred.

• Outstanding track record in marketing and leasing of retail properties

• Strong negotiation and communication skills, with the ability to build rapport with diverse stakeholders

​

ad.title: Cleaning Attendant
ad.description:
About Us

Agape Services is a Christian social enterprise under Far East Organization, whose aim is to provide meaningful employment and benefits to people with disadvantaged background, as a demonstration of the love and teachings of Jesus Christ.

Agape Services offer services in Agape Cleaning, Agape Laundry as well as moving and disposal. We aim to provide employment through our services to the disadvantaged who would otherwise not be able to cope in a regular work environment.



Responsibilities


  • Provide cleaning services at office premises, residential apartments and/or building areas
  • Carry out general cleaning and maintain cleanliness using appropriate materials, products and equipment
  • Handle and dispose waste according to standard procedures, instructions and cleaning industry standards
  • Comply with performance standards as well as workplace safety and health policy and procedures


Requirements

  • Able to understand basic instructions in English and communicate to customers
  • Must be able to carry out cleaning duties such as dusting, wiping, sweeping, mopping, handling and disposing waste
  • Diligent, efficient and well-mannered worker, willing to learn and be trained



Receive a $500 Sign-On Reward!
Stay with us for 12 months to qualify. Terms and conditions apply.

ad.title: Assistant Manager, Asset Management
ad.description:
About Us


Far East Organization is a Christian Enterprise, which develops real estate and operates businesses by serving with grace, love, integrity and honesty. Together with its Hong Kong-based sister company Sino Group, they are one of Asia’s largest real estate groups, with operations in Singapore, Malaysia, Australia, Japan, China (Mainland and Hong Kong) and USA.
