ad.title: Project Manager (Built Environment)
ad.description:
Job Duties and Responsibilities

  • Work with cross-functional teams of Technical Officers and Managers to oversee project works involving MEP, building, structural, civil works in an Integrated Facilities Management (IFM) Contract and ensure that KPIs are met
  • Responsible for preliminary survey, feasibility studies and working with consultants, vendors, users to determine requirements, preparing cost estimates to derive project budgets for various multi-disciplinary projects.
  • Ensure that project technical specifications, regulatory compliances, code of practises, schedule of work and all necessary documentations are obtained, and project milestones are met.
  • Responsible for developing detailed project plans, budgets, schedule, ensure that project technical specifications, regulatory compliances, code of practises, all necessary documentations are obtained, project milestones are met and ensuring high quality workmanship are delivered.
  • Attend regular maintenance meetings on behalf of the Company with Clients and Clients’ Representatives.
  • Periodic review for on-going project to identify risks, verifying stages of work, present reports, post-project evaluation and finalize completion of works.


Required Qualifications

  • At least 5 years relevant working experience in Project Management works involving engineering / A&A projects.
  • Recognized Degree in Building/Mechanical/Electrical Engineering, Construction Management, Project Management, Facilities Management, Quantity Surveyor or equivalent.
  • Strong understanding of M&E systems, building infrastructure, and maintenance best practices and ability to read / interpret technical drawings, schematics.
  • Excellent leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills
  • Knowledge in BIM, AutoCAD and/or Revit MEP advantageous.

ad.title: Maintenance Engineer (Security System)
ad.description:
Roles & Responsibilities

Key Roles and Responsibilities

  • To troubleshoot and maintain/service security systems at clients’ premises
  • Attend to fault calls during/after office hours
  • Support Maintenance Lead to upkeep Clients’ security system inventory list and maintenance spares
  • Support Maintenance Lead to liaise with clients, integrated facility management representatives and trade contractors so as to facilitate to carry out work at clients’ premises
  • Liaise closely with clients, integrated facility management representatives on all service/maintenance related issues to achieve optimal results.
  • Ensure that works carried out are in compliance with the Workplace Safety and Health Act.
  • Perform any other technical tasks as required including new installations, A&A, HOTO, T&C and equipment replacement etc

Requirements

  • Diploma/Higher Nitec/Nitec in Engineering/IT or relevant working experience in the related field
  • Hands on experience in security systems such as Integrated Security Management Systems, Card Access Control Systems, Intrusion Detection Systems, CCTV systems and Electronic Key Management System.
  • Additional knowledge in hardware configuration, System Networking and integration with third-party systems will be an advantage
  • Must be a team player,Independent and willing to take ownership and Pro-active in Problem solving
  • Driving License (Car or Motorbike) – Preferably if candidate possesses its own vehicle
  • Singapore Citizens are preferred
  • Work Location is at 3 Shan Road/Clients’ premises
  • 5 days work week
  • Require to perform rotational standby duties
  • Experience Candidate will be considered for Maintenance Engineer
  • Fresh Graduates keen to seek a career path in security system are welcome to apply as a Maintenance Technician

ad.title: Accounts Clerk
ad.description:
Roles & Responsibilities

Responsibilities

  • Record daily financial transactions, including accounts payable invoices and general ledger journal entries, accurately and promptly in the accounting system.
  • Prepare fixed assets schedule. 
  • Generate monthly inventory reports and maintain accurate inventory records.
  • Liaise with internal teams and external parties to resolve accounting discrepancies.
  • Other duties as assigned by the Superior

Requirements

  • Diploma in Accounting

  • Good knowledge of Microsoft Office Excel and Words; able to work independently with minimum supervision and meet deadlines

ad.title: Technician
ad.description:
Key Responsibilities

  • Troubleshooting & Repair: Diagnose mechanical, electrical, and hydraulic system failures (pumps, motors, valves, actuators) and perform corrective actions.
  • Maintenance: Perform routine preventative maintenance, including oil changes, filter replacements, oiling, and greasing of machines.
  • Technical Inspection: Inspect equipment for leaks, wear, or damage and conduct tests to ensure functionality.
  • Component Overhaul: Overhaul hydraulic components such as pumps, cylinders, and motors.
  • Safety Compliance: Execute lockout/tag-out (LOTO) procedures and comply with Workplace Safety and Health (WSH) regulations.
  • Documentation: Maintain accurate service records, maintenance logs, and job status reports.


Requirements & Qualifications

  • Education: NITEC/Diploma in Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or related fields.
  • Experience: 1–3+ years of relevant experience in hydraulic, diesel machinery, or heavy equipment maintenance.
  • Skills: Proficiency with hand/power tools, ability to read hydraulic/electrical circuit diagrams, and knowledge of troubleshooting
  • Physical Ability: Must be capable of working in workshops, shipyards, or on-site, sometimes in demanding conditions
Location
  • ​80 Lim Chu Kang Lane 1

ad.title: Mechanical Technician
ad.description:
Overview

Element has an opportunity for a Mechanical Technician to join our growing team. This is a great opportunity to develop your career within a Global TIC business.


Responsibilities

  • Interpretation of internal and client requirements
  • Supervising allocated staff and being responsible for the day to day management of the relevant discipline
  • Operating complex mechanical testing equipment
  • Maintaining accurate and systematic records
  • Performing other duties to fulfil the demands of the laboratory
  • Performing general duties in accordance with Element's internal procedures, quality as well as Health and Safety system
  • Application of instrumentation and the setting up of complex data capture equipment
  • Report writing, data interpretation and analysis

Skills / Qualifications

  • Educated to a good general standard including English and Maths
  • Diploma in Mechanical Engineering or relevant / experience in related (testing laboratory)
  • Hands-on job
  • Sound mechanical bias
  • Previous experience in an appropriate Engineering environment is a distinct advantage
  • Experience of working within a Mechanical testing environment is advantageous
  • An understanding of a recognised Quality Management Systems such as ISO 17025 is beneficial
  • Self motivated Technician with the ability to multi task, organise and work under the pressure of a busy commercial mechanical test laboratory
  • Excellent attention to detail
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • A team player attitude
  • Excellent analytical and problem solving skills

#LI-CL1


Company Overview

Element is one of the fastest growing testing, inspection and certification businesses in the world. Globally we have more than 9,000 brilliant minds operating from 270 sites across 30 countries. Together we share an ambitious purpose to ‘Make tomorrow safer than today’.

When failure in use is not an option, we help customers make certain that their products, materials, processes and services are safe, compliant and fit for purpose. From early R&D, through complex regulatory approvals and into production, our global laboratory network of scientists, engineers, and technologists support customers to achieve assurance over product quality, sustainable outcomes, and market access.

While we are proud of our global reach, working at Element feels like being part of a smaller company. We empower you to take charge of your career, and reward excellence and integrity with growth and development.

Industries across the world depend on our care, attention to detail and the absolute accuracy of our work. The role we have to play in creating a safer world is much bigger than our organization.

Diversity Statement

At Element, we always take pride in putting our people first. We are an equal opportunity employer that recognizes diversity and inclusion as fundamental to our Vision of becoming “the world’s most trusted testing partner”.

 

All suitably qualified candidates will receive consideration for employment on the basis of objective work related criteria and without regard for the following: age, disability, ethnic origin, gender, marital status, race, religion, responsibility of dependents, sexual orientation, or gender identity or other characteristics in accordance with the applicable governing laws or other characteristics in accordance with the applicable governing laws.

ad.title: Chemistry Technician
ad.description:
Overview

Element has an opportunity for a Chemistry Technician for testing, sample preparation and delivery/collection of samples and preparation of test reports.

Responsibilities

  • Analysis of water-physical, chemical and microbiological parameters
  • Analysis of ores and minerals
  • Analysis of water, soil, aggregates, concrete and scale samples
  • Operation of equipment like pH / EC / TDS meters, UV-visible spectrophotometers, ovens, furnaces, incubators, fusion machines
  • Prepare the reagents, standard solution and ensure standardisation of the solution used in the laboratory
  • Handling and storage all chemicals as per the Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS)
  • Managing the calibration of glass wares and the instruments
  • Performance of qualitative and quantitative laboratory tests and analysis of other substances as per standard method
  • Issue reports and receive samples to and from the respective entities
  • Involvement in site and field testing with various international standards or in-house procedures
  • Ensure work compliance to ISO 17025 requirements
  • Coordinating with the other technicians in the team to carry out work as per the planning schedule
  • Manual handling of samples
  • General maintenance, cleaning and checking of equipment
  • Working to all HSE standards
  • Interaction with clients during witnessed testing
  • Generating test reports for clients (data entry, quality assurance)
  • Ensuring compliance to all Element Material Technology quality standards
  • Driving to customer site to collect samples and / or conduct testing at their lab on a regular / daily basis
  • Willing to assist in other activities from time to time that may be requested by the department manager to assist in the execution of Element Material Technology annual targets and budget

Skills / Qualifications

  • Have a diploma / degree in chemistry with at least 2 years experience in similar role
  • Have the ability to work in teams under pressure and flexible to work different working hours (night / day) shifts if required or necessary
  • Good English communication skills: reading, writing, speaking
  • Good maths skills
  • Knowledge and experience of safe operation of testing equipment and operating in an independent / chemistry / consultant laboratory

ad.title: Associate Test Technician
ad.description:
Overview

Element has an opportunity for Associate Test Technician to join our rapidly expanding team. As a member of the operations team in one of the various laboratories across Element, the Associate Test Technician position performs a variety of routine and non-routine testing assignments within the department assigned and insures timely and accurate test results under direct supervision.

The technician positions must be proficient in a number of core business skills including but not limited to compliance to established safety rules, code of conduct, and company policies and procedures.


Responsibilities

  • Perform varies of construction testing onsite such as concrete, soil, structural fixings and etc.
  • Perform test as directed by customer specification / Engineer’s instruction
  • Operate testing independently and /or under supervision of senior test technician
  • Gather and record test data and able to communicates properly with client onsite.
  • Prepare and compile reports/worksheets as per supervisor’s instruction

Skills / Qualifications

  • Working knowledge of laboratory safety & site safety.
  • HS diploma or GED certificate preferred. College or vocational school coursework welcomed
  • 0-2 years’ experience in a testing related field
  • Basic understanding of testing concepts
  • Possess computer skills.
  • Ability to apply concepts of basic algebra and geometry a plus

Physical Demands

  • Frequency of required physical force: Minimum (moderate).
  • Lifting requirements: lifting <50 lbs. often and > 50 lbs. on occasion
  • Ability to stand on feet for long periods of time
  • Ability to lift 50+lbs periodically

#LI-CL1

ad.title: Head of Calibration
ad.description:
Overview

Element has an opportunity for a Head of Calibration. In this role you will be reporting to the Division Director and be responsible for all Element business operations and processes within the business unit.


Responsibilities

  • Execution & Results: Ensure compliance with corporate policies and practices for Health, Safety & Environment and Quality; Profit and Loss responsibility and accountability for laboratory financial performance; Drive and Execute in relation to operational performance; Deliver results in line with forecasts and expectations; Ensure that customer’s technical, quality and delivery requirements are satisfied.

  • Strategy: Formulate and execute a strategy to grow the business with a primary focus on organic growth; Ensure development of technical services and processes in support of growth strategy with prime focus on testing services relating to the environmental chemistry sector.

  • Sales/Commercial/Go To Market Strategy: Build and maintain effective relations with clients to enhance the laboratory’s profitability. Work with the sales team to set targets and to ensure budgeted sales are achieved; Actively support, and where necessary lead major customer proposals and contracts; Serve as a key relationship partner for high level customer engagements. Ensure that reports and quotes satisfy requirements for contract review, technical content, and minimize legal risk.

  • Process/Performance Improvement: Enhance skills and processes across the businesses through a focus on continuous improvement; effectively deploy a management tool box with analysis to drive appropriate KPI’s/metric improvement.

  • Leadership/People Management: Develop and build a high performing team; Build technical strength and ensure all staff are adequately trained to perform their duties. Create a culture of ‘can do’ and ensure high levels of staff engagement and productivity. Provide regular, timely communications and feedback to employees. Provide dynamic hands-on leadership to team to ensure a consistent application of standards, values, and improvement tools.


Roles and Responsibilities

1) Management System

  • Monthly reporting on lab P&L and other KPIs.

  • Adhere to Element Quality and SHE management system policies and procedures.

  • Assist with hazard identification and risk assessment exercises within their areas of responsibility.

  • Monitor day-to-day health and safety in area of responsibility and ensure all hazards, near misses and accidents are reported.

2) Technical and Business Development Responsibilities

  • Provide technical expertise to the team on the services provided by the company to its customers.

  • Plan daily operation for execution as per agreed timelines with customers.

  • Train the technical personnel in the job requirements.

  • Take responsibility for the budgeted numbers and strive to overachieve the targets month after month.

  • Strive and achieve 100% customer satisfaction by timely reports, by providing technical advice to customers, when needed and proactively anticipate and satisfy the customer needs.

  • Be abreast of the latest developments in the field.

  • Inter-department/location co-ordination and work as “One Company One Team” both internally and externally.

  • Provide timely reports on the activities and future outlook for the business.

  • Any other duties and responsibilities assigned and agreed from time to time.



3) Managerial Responsibilities

  • Work with other functions and employees to maximize employee retention.

  • Conduct regular team meetings with specific emphasis on HSE, technical updates, etc.

  • Define training plans for the team.

  • Motivate the team for superior performance and be a conduit between employee’s needs and the company.

  • Carries out supervisory responsibilities in accordance with the organization's policies and applicable laws.

  • Departmental responsibilities include interviewing, hiring, and training employees; planning, assigning, and directing work; appraising performance; rewarding and disciplining employees; addressing complaints and resolving conflicts.


Skills / Qualifications

  • Education: Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Metrology, or a related technical discipline.

  • Experience: Minimum 5–8 years of experience in quality, calibration, metrology, or testing laboratory management.

  • Accreditation: Proven experience managing ISO/IEC 17025 accredited laboratories is advantageous.

  • Technical Background: Background in dimensional, electrical, temperature, or pressure calibration.

  • Sector Knowledge: Proven experience in aerospace quality requirement and experiences is advantageous.

  • Expertise: In-depth knowledge of calibration principles, uncertainty analysis, and traceability.

  • Soft Skills: Excellent leadership, teamwork, communication, and organizational skills.

  • Systems: Proficient in calibration software and laboratory information management systems (LIMS).

  • Abilities: Strong analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making abilities; Customer-oriented mindset with strong project management capability.

  • Financial Literacy: Finance knowledge on budgeting and planning; Departmental revenue and finance KPIs.

Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)

  • On-time delivery of calibration jobs.

  • Compliance with ISO/IEC 17025 and audit performance.

  • Customer satisfaction scores.

  • Staff competency and retention.

  • Efficiency improvements and cost control.


Company Overview

Element is one of the fastest growing testing, inspection and certification businesses in the world. Globally we have more than 9,000 brilliant minds operating from 270 sites across 30 countries. Together we share an ambitious purpose to ‘Make tomorrow safer than today’.

When failure in use is not an option, we help customers make certain that their products, materials, processes and services are safe, compliant and fit for purpose. From early R&D, through complex regulatory approvals and into production, our global laboratory network of scientists, engineers, and technologists support customers to achieve assurance over product quality, sustainable outcomes, and market access.

All suitably qualified candidates will receive consideration for employment on the basis of objective work related criteria and without regard for the following: age, disability, ethnic origin, gender, marital status, race, religion, responsibility of dependents, sexual orientation, or gender identity or other characteristics in accordance with the applicable governing laws.

ad.title: Engineer
ad.description:
Overview

Element has an opportunity for an Engineer to join our rapidly expanding team. As a member of the operations team, the Engineer position performs a variety of routine and non-routine testing assignments within the department assigned and insures timely and accurate test results under minimal direct supervision.

The engineer position must be proficient in a number of core business and technical skills including but not limited to compliance to established safety rules, code of conduct, and company policies and procedures.


Responsibilities

  • In charge of operation of NDT testing for Concrete buildings materials includes test arrangement, testing and reporting.
  • Provides counsel to coworkers and customers requiring critical analysis of data.
  • Be responsible for SINGLAS Accreditation.
  • Assist in the professional development of novice technicians and engineers
  • Utilizes an assortment of skills, methods, procedures and materials in advanced testing analysis.
  • Capable to read/translate construction drawings and technical requirement in reaching testing conclusions.
  • Confident making both formal and information presentations on conventional and/or routine testing to a wide range of stakeholders
  • Review test report and ensure all laboratory are in constant state of regulatory compliance and inspection readiness.
  • Develop training material and deliver technical training to laboratory colleagues.

Skills / Qualifications

  • Possess minimum a Bachelor Degree in Civil Engineering or Material Science. Fresh graduate is welcome.
  • Working knowledge of construction site and laboratory safety will be beneficial.
  • Possesses a wealth of knowledge, deep awareness and critical understanding related to the scope, core theories, concepts, terminologies and boundaries of civil engineering discipline
  • Has a depth of specialty knowledge and understanding in multiple areas of civil engineering discipline
  • Possesses a demonstrated ability to apply academic knowledge within the context of Element’s business
  • Possess strong computer skills and auto-cad.
  • Ability to apply concepts of basic algebra and geometry a plus
  • Possess class 3 driving license is a plus

ad.title: Driver
ad.description:
Overview
Element has an opportunity for a Driver Class 3 to be a part of the transportation department whose primary responsibility will be aiding in logistics and operation.

Responsibilities

  • Deliver and collect samples, invoices reports to and from the respective entities
  • Visit bank / visa centres / ministry / health centers to handle the necessary transactions on a regular basis

ad.description:

Element has an opportunity for an Associate Engineer to join our rapidly expanding team. As a member of the operations team, the Engineer position performs a variety of routine and non-routine testing assignments within the department assigned and insures timely and accurate test results under minimal direct supervision.

This role is based in Tuas Loop, Singapore.

  • Closely involved – as a contributing team member – with a variety of assignments with the laboratory
  • Accountable for technical activities of a limited scope and may attain additional responsibilities for release of standard and/or routine data to clients
  • Conduct Routine Tests such as stripping test, moisture content, rebar’s geometrical measurements
  • Work with Engineer and lead with advanced tests, including tensile test and ad-hoc experiments.
  • Accurately record and report data from tests.

ad.title: Data Processor (MY)
ad.description:
Overview

Element has an opportunity for a Data Processor to join our rapidly expanding team. As a member of the operations team in one of the various laboratories across Element, the Data Processer position primarily responsible for assisting in designing, planning, and coordinating geotechnical instrumentation projects, scheduling control, obtaining permits for on-site installation, and setting up and commissioning monitoring.

This role is based in Johor Bahru, Malaysia.


Responsibilities

  • Receive and review field data (borehole logs, CPT, SPT, rock coring, etc.) from site engineers/ geologist.
  • Compile lab test results (e.g., moisture content, Atterberg limits, triaxial, consolidation tests).
  • Validate data accuracy against site records and laboratory logs
  • Generate borehole logs using software such as AutoCAD, Geosmart
  • Work closely with geotechnical engineers, drafters, lab technicians, and field staff & follow up with external laboratories or drilling contractors for missing data or clarifications.
  • Data processing for field test such as Permeability Test, Packer Test, Pressuremeter Test, Vane Shear Test, CPT
  • Align reports with project specifications, national standards (e.g., ASTM, BS, IS codes), and local regulatory requirements
  • Strong attention to detail and ability to spot inconsistencies in data.
  • Familiarity with common soil types in Singapore
  • Ability to work under pressure to meet submission deadlines
  • Update project managers on report status and highlight any delays or issues
  • Support revisions and feedback from engineers and clients promptly and professionally
  • Perform quality checks on all data inputs and report outputs

Skills / Qualifications

  • Minimum Degree in Civil Engineering or Geotechnical Engineering or Geology
  • Working experience in geotechnical instrumentation and site investigation works would be advantageous
  • Able to withstand the challenging working conditions
  • Strong and determined quality focus with indefatigable determination to get the job done under the most challenging conditions
  • Self-motivated team player with excellent organisational and communication skills


#LI-CL1
#LI-PN1

ad.title: Instrumentation Engineer
ad.description:
Overview

Element has an opportunity for an Instrumentation Engineer to join our rapidly expanding team. In this role, you will perform a variety of routine and non-routine testing assignments within the department assigned and insures timely and accurate test results under minimal direct supervision.

This role is based in Kaki Bukit, Singapore.

Responsibilities

  • Provide technical and engineering leadership, and supports the development of new technologies within a local laboratory
  • Be accountable for accurate and timely release of testing data to customers including sign-offs of quality control data as acceptable
  • Be expected to obtain guidance in non-routine situation testing movement, vibration, real time reading
  • Have the ability to oversee standard and/or routine technical problem solving activities
  • Interact with customers on technical issues and questions
  • Provide Input and support on business development opportunities with new or existing customers
  • Be confident in making both formal and informal presentations on conventional and/or routine testing to a wide range of stakeholders
  • Assist in the professional development of novice technicians and engineers within the local laboratory
  • Carry out crucial examination and implementation of testing concepts and data
  • Utilize an assortment of skill, methods, procedures and materials in advanced testing analysis
  • Be adept at drawing on internal knowledge as well as industry and/or academic resources in reaching testing conclusion

Skills / Qualifications

  • Possess a Degree in Civil / Mechanical / Electrical / Geotechnical Engineering with at least 3 years of working experience
  • Working knowledge of site safety
  • Possess a high level of expertise related to scope, core theories, concepts, terminologies and boundaries of chosen engineering discipline - Water, ground and environment
  • Possesses a demonstrated ability to apply academic knowledge within the context of Element’s business
  • In-depth understanding of testing concepts
  • Can analyze and compiles testing data from the tests performed
  • Possess strong computer skills

Element is one of the fastest growing testing, inspection and certification businesses in the world. Globally we have more than 9,000 brilliant minds operating from 270 sites across 30 countries. Together we share an ambitious purpose to ‘Make tomorrow safer than today’.

When failure in use is not an option, we help customers make certain that their products, materials, processes and services are safe, compliant and fit for purpose. From early R&D, through complex regulatory approvals and into production, our global laboratory network of scientists, engineers, and technologists support customers to achieve assurance over product quality, sustainable outcomes, and market access.

While we are proud of our global reach, working at Element feels like being part of a smaller company. We empower you to take charge of your career, and reward excellence and integrity with growth and development.

Industries across the world depend on our care, attention to detail and the absolute accuracy of our work. The role we have to play in creating a safer world is much bigger than our organization.

Diversity Statement

​

At Element, we always take pride in putting our people first. We are an equal opportunity employer that recognizes diversity and inclusion as fundamental to our Vision of becoming “the world’s most trusted testing partner”.

 

All suitably qualified candidates will receive consideration for employment on the basis of objective work related criteria and without regard for the following: age, disability, ethnic origin, gender, marital status, race, religion, responsibility of dependents, sexual orientation, or gender identity or other characteristics in accordance with the applicable governing laws or other characteristics in accordance with the applicable governing laws.

 

The contractor will not discharge or in any other manner discriminate against employees or applicants because they have inquired about, discussed, or disclosed their own pay or the pay of another employee or applicant. However, employees who have access to the compensation information of other employees or applicants as a part of their essential job functions cannot disclose the pay of other employees or applicants to individuals who do not otherwise have access to compensation information, unless the disclosure is (a) in response to a formal complaint or charge, (b) in furtherance of an investigation, proceeding, hearing, or action, including an investigation conducted by the employer, or (c) consistent with the contractor’s legal duty to furnish information. 41 CFR 60-1.35(c)

ad.title: Data Processor (Instrumentation)
ad.description:
Overview

Element has an opportunity for Data Processor whose primary responsibility is to serve as a regional expert in expert of analytical instrumentation, analyzing various matrixes, installation, servicing & maintenance of sophisticated analytical instruments.

This role is based in Kaki Bukit Singapore.

Responsibilities

  • Collect, process, and validate geotechnical/structural monitoring data.
  • Maintain data records, prepare reports, and highlight anomalies.
  • Operate data loggers and monitoring systems; troubleshoot as needed.
  • Coordinate with field teams for data retrieval and equipment checks.

#LI-CL1

Skills / Qualifications

  • Diploma/Degree in Engineering, Geotechnical, Instrumentation, or related field
  • Proficient in Excel; familiarity with instrumentation software an advantage
  • Detail-oriented with good analytical and communication skills
  • Experience in construction or infrastructure monitoring preferred

ad.title: Calibration Officer
ad.description:
Overview

Element has an opportunity for a Calibration Technician to join our rapidly expanding team. As a member of the operations team, the Senior Test Technician position performs a variety of routine and non-routine testing assignments within the department assigned and insures timely and accurate test results under minimal direct supervision.

The technician positions must be proficient in a number of core business skills including but not limited to compliance to established safety rules, code of conduct, and company policies and procedures.

Responsibilities

  • Performs duties comparable to that of a Senior Technician, utilizing the added skills and knowledge that comes with earning an Associate’s or bachelor’s degree in a testing-related field
  • Designs, sets-up and performs a variety of test according to customer specifications and requirements
  • Requires very little supervision and technical input of a Department Manager, Principal Technician, Project Coordinator or Engineer
  • Possess the ability to mentor and train technicians who have less experience through effective communication of technical data
  • Ability to transfer technical knowledge/requirements and successfully satisfy and meet customer specifications

Skills / Qualifications

  • Working knowledge of laboratory safety
  • HS diploma or GED certificate preferred. College or vocational school coursework welcomed
  • Possesses a recognized scientific or engineering qualification, or significant experience in a testing related field through which an equivalent level of knowledge has been attained
  • In-depth understanding of testing concepts for electronic and temperature calibration
  • Analyzes and compiles testing data from the tests performed
  • Possess strong computer skills
  • Ability to apply concepts of basic algebra and geometry a plus

Physical Demands

  • Frequency of required physical force: Minimum (moderate)
  • Lifting requirements: lifting <50 lbs. often and >50 lbs. on occasion
  • Ability to stand on feet for long periods of time
  • Ability to lift 50+lbs periodically

#LI-CL1

Company Overview

ad.title: Quantity Surveyor
ad.description:
Overview

Element has an opportunity for a Quantity Surveyor to join our growing team. This is a great opportunity to develop your career within a Global TIC business.

This role is based in Johor Bahru

Responsibilities

  • Prepare technical and commercial proposals during tender stage including bills of quantities material quantity and taking off quantities, attend site show round, site visit, attend tender briefing, seeks clarifications with respective client/ consultant if any.
  • Study details and discuss with management, attend tender interviews including method statement, technical proposal presentation and tender clarification.
  • Manpower and resources budgeting, monthly monitoring, review and update.
  • Prepare pricing of bill of quantity and re-measurement works.
  • Assist in establishing a client's requirements and coordinate quality controls to ensure deliverables meet requirements.
  • Monitor tender/quotation results, check tender/quotation status via Gebiz and inform management; for private tender/quotation, to follow-up closely with client.
  • Collaborate with client for kick off meeting and attend kick off meeting with project team Prepare handing over and brief project details scope with project team, review contract documents.
  • Evaluate and handle all contractual issues, variation orders, insurances/bond and render support/advice to project team.
  • Conduct cost analysis, manage project timeline and cost conduct commercial negotiations.
  • Attending site visit to monitor site progress and performance.
  • In charge of preparing progress claim submission, variation orders to client and handling subcontractors’ claims.
  • Call for a quotation from supplier and/or subcon if necessary.
  • Assist in material/equipment procurement, inventory, and general administration task other duties as may be assigned on a need basis requirement.
  • Preparation of technical details & costing for tender submission.
  • Handle progress claims and progress payment, verifying payment to sub-contractors, ensuring all variations to contract are captured and tracking variation orders.
  • Conduct cost analysis, manage project timeline and cost, conduct commercial negotiations.
  • Liaise and establish rapport with consultants, quantity surveyors, developers, and contractors on certification of claims for payments.
  • Other ad-hoc duties as assigned.

Skills / Qualifications

  • Diploma or Degree in Quantity Surveying, Civil Engineering, or related field.
  • Minimum 2–3 years in QS or cost management roles; experience in building or M&E projects preferred.

Skills:

  • Strong knowledge of cost estimation, tendering, and contract administration.
  • Familiarity with standard forms of contract
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel) and QS software
  • Excellent analytical, negotiation, and communication skills.
  • Professional Membership preferred

#LI-CL1

Company Overview

ad.title: Technician (Calibration)
ad.description:
Overview

Element has an opportunity for an Associate Engineer to join our rapidly expanding team. As a member of the operations team, the Engineer position performs a variety of routine and non-routine testing assignments within the department assigned and insures timely and accurate test results under minimal direct supervision. 

 

This role is based in Ang Mo Kio, Singapore.

Responsibilities

  • Perform Calibration and Measurement of Dimension gauges and instruments as per WI using CMM, reference master, tester etc (on-site and in-house) with report and necessary administrative work
  • Conduct routine maintenance and calibration of lab equipment as schedule and assigned
  • Ensure proper delivery of service to customer(s)
  • Help manager to manage day-to-day calibration work in the labs reference to ISO17025 standard, eg. Quality/ Job Planning/ Job Assignment/ Customer Requirements etc
  • Any other duties as assigned by Manager/ Section Head/ Senior staff

Skills / Qualifications


• ITE/GCE/ Diploma in Aerospace/ Precision / Mechanical Engineering etc
• 2 to 3 years working knowledge in QC/QA or Calibration / Testing field.
• Ability to read and understand technical drawing and GD&T.
• Ability to analyze and compile Calibration / testing data from the Calibration/ tests performed
• Good to have 2 to 3 years CMM knowledge.
• Good to have ISO17025 and measurement uncertainty knowledge
• Possess strong computer skills
• Basic Mathematical Skills. Ability to apply concepts of basic algebra and geometry a plus


 


Physical Demands:
• Frequency of required physical force: Minimum (moderate)
• Lifting requirements: lifting <50 lbs. often and >50 lbs. on occasion
• Ability to stand on feet for long periods of time
• Ability to lift 50+lbs periodically
